message:
docs(AGENTS.md): add bv tool guidance for AI-assisted task planning

This commit extends the AGENTS.md documentation with a new section on using
the `bv` terminal UI as an AI sidecar for Beads-based project management.

## What is bv?

bv is a fast terminal UI for Beads projects that reads the `.beads/beads.jsonl`
task database. It provides:

- Interactive list/detail rendering of issues
- Precomputed dependency graph metrics (PageRank, critical path, cycles)
- Instant visibility into blockers and execution order

## Why document this for agents?

Agents working with Beads task lists often need to:

1. Determine which tasks are ready to start (no blockers)
2. Understand task dependencies and priority
3. Plan parallel work tracks
4. Detect cycles or bottlenecks

Without guidance, agents might attempt to parse the JSONL directly or
"hallucinate" dependency traversal logic. The bv robot flags provide
deterministic, pre-computed outputs that agents can trust.

## Robot flags documented

| Flag | Output | Use Case |
|------|--------|----------|
| `--robot-help` | All AI-facing commands | Discovery |
| `--robot-insights` | PageRank, betweenness, HITS, critical path, cycles | Quick triage |
| `--robot-plan` | Parallel tracks, unblocks lists | Execution planning |
| `--robot-priority` | Priority recommendations with reasoning | Task selection |
| `--robot-recipes` | Available filter presets | Workflow setup |
| `--robot-diff` | Changes since commit/date | Progress tracking |

## Integration with existing AGENTS.md content

This section follows the existing "ast-grep vs ripgrep" guidance pattern:
providing clear, actionable advice on when to use specialized tools versus
general-purpose approaches. It complements the existing Beads integration
section by offering a concrete tool recommendation for graph-aware operations.

The key message: "Use these commands instead of hand-rolling graph logic;
bv already computes the hard parts so agents can act safely and quickly."

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -267,3 +267,18 @@ rg -l -t ts 'useQuery\(' | xargs ast-grep run -l TypeScript -p 'useQuery($A)' -r
 * Unit of match: `ast-grep` = node; `rg` = line.
 * False positives: `ast-grep` low; `rg` depends on your regex.
 * Rewrites: `ast-grep` first-class; `rg` requires ad‑hoc sed/awk and risks collateral edits.
+
+---
+
+### Using bv as an AI sidecar
+
+bv is a fast terminal UI for Beads projects (.beads/beads.jsonl). It renders lists/details and precomputes dependency metrics (PageRank, critical path, cycles, etc.) so you instantly see blockers and execution order. For agents, it’s a graph sidecar: instead of parsing JSONL or risking hallucinated traversal, call the robot flags to get deterministic, dependency-aware outputs.
+
+- bv --robot-help — shows all AI-facing commands.
+- bv --robot-insights — JSON graph metrics (PageRank, betweenness, HITS, critical path, cycles) with top-N summaries for quick triage.
+- bv --robot-plan — JSON execution plan: parallel tracks, items per track, and unblocks lists showing what each item frees up.
+- bv --robot-priority — JSON priority recommendations with reasoning and confidence.
+- bv --robot-recipes — list recipes (default, actionable, blocked, etc.); apply via bv --recipe <name> to pre-filter/sort before other flags.
+- bv --robot-diff --diff-since <commit|date> — JSON diff of issue changes, new/closed items, and cycles introduced/resolved.
+
+Use these commands instead of hand-rolling graph logic; bv already computes the hard parts so agents can act safely and quickly.
